aggregation_rule: Candidate superiority requires positive route/outcome movement and zero hard-stop failures.

abstention_quality.hard_stop: abstention collapse or unsafe forced routing

consequence_visibility.hard_stop: receipt omits why a route was chosen, abstained, or rejected

control_preservation.hard_stop: fallback/static baseline, fail-closed behavior, or R1-R5 replay condition degrades
control_preservation.superiority: not independently sufficient; all controls must pass

mirror_masked_invariance.hard_stop: masked, mirrored, or order-variant inputs change verdict without lawful reason

no_regression.hard_stop: any R1-R5 pass condition, trust-zone validation, or package deferral boundary regresses

outcome_delta.hard_stop: any useful-output regression on protected control cases
outcome_delta.superiority: candidate useful-output success exceeds best static control under same inputs

overrouting_penalty.hard_stop: candidate routes when static hold/abstention should dominate

route_superiority.defer: candidate evidence incomplete, comparator mutated, or input family not preregistered
route_superiority.meaningful_movement: candidate improves route quality without any hard-stop failure
route_superiority.no_movement: candidate matches static control or only shifts route distribution without outcome gain
route_superiority.superiority: candidate must beat both static controls on preregistered route-quality cases
